newsSince: Oxford Instruments published its FY2026 Annual Report and notice of AGM around June 9, 2026, confirming the previously reported preliminary results: Advanced Technologies orders +28%, group gross margin lifted to ~51% post-NanoScience/quantum divestment, and FY2027 guidance targeting ~20% operating margin with high-teens AT growth. No genuinely new earnings or contract event since June 9; the report formalizes already-known prelims.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-supply-chain lens: FY2026 report confirms Advanced Technologies orders +28% and margin uplift after quantum divestment, keeping the under-covered semi/metrology/cryogenics leverage intact. Confirmatory not new, so a small positive nudge; YTD re-rating handled separately.
verdicts.bull: AT orders +28%, ~51% gross margin and FY27 20% op-margin target give clean, under-owned exposure to AI-hardware R&D, metrology and compound-semi tools.
verdicts.neutral: Strong prelims now formalized in annual report; no fresh catalyst since June 9 and Imaging recovery still gradual.
verdicts.bear: Group revenue fell ~15% YoY post-divestments; cyclical scientific-tools demand and a +43% YTD run leave limited margin of safety.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Advanced Technologies order momentum and FY27 margin expansion
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical/scientific-tools demand softness; rich valuation after strong YTD
